title: Fix build error with GCC by forcing Clang in CMake on android/aarch64
body:
GCC does not recognize Clang-specific warning flags like -Wunreachable-code-break and -Wunreachable-code-return, which are passed by upstream submodules (e.g., ggml). This patch forces CMake to use Clang via command-line arguments, avoiding the need to patch nested submodules.

This resolves compiler errors without modifying submodule source code.

step_plan.summary: Force clang/clang++ as the C/C++ compilers in top-level CMake before project() when building for Android aarch64 (unless the user explicitly set compilers). Add a clarifying comment to the later compiler-ID conditional so it remains aligned with the pre-project forcing logic.

steps.reason: CMake chooses compilers when project() is processed. To ensure all subdirectories and upstream submodules (e.g., ggml) get Clang on Android aarch64, set CMAKE_C_COMPILER and CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER (via CACHE FILEPATH ... FORCE) before project() runs, but only when the user has not explicitly provided compilers. This prevents GCC from being used and failing on Clang-only flags.
steps.side_effects: Changes which compiler CMake will probe and use for the entire build when targeting Android/aarch64. If clang/clang++ are not available on PATH, configuration may fail; cross-toolchain files that already set compilers may interact (the block must avoid overriding explicit -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER/-DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER). Requires running Android/aarch64 builds in CI and local testing; non-Android builds should be unaffected.

steps.operation: Leave/validate post-project compiler-detection conditional (no functional change) and add a clarifying comment
steps.reason: The existing conditional that inspects CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID / CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID (e.g., to add -fpermissive for GNU) must still execute after project() and reflect the compilers that were forced earlier. Add a short comment referencing the pre-project forcing block to make the dependency explicit and avoid future regressions.
steps.side_effects: No behavioral change intended; ensures existing logic continues to only add GCC-specific flags when compilers are actually GNU. Tests: ensure the conditional yields the expected result for Android/aarch64 (should not detect GNU if Clang was forced) and for systems with GCC. Downstream modules that rely on CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID will be evaluated based on the forced compiler.
